title: Triaged Findings
description: Output of the triage step in ops-pr-respond. Splits a flat findings array (produced by aggregate over the audit-* fan-out) into actionable, deferred, and rejected lists. The actionable list is the iterate input for the impl-finding sub-pipeline.

actionable.description: Findings that will be addressed in this run via the impl-finding sub-pipeline.

id.description: Stable identifier for this finding within the run. Used as the iterate key and referenced in commit messages.

file.description: Repo-relative path to the file the fix should touch. Must not start with '/' (no absolute paths). Allowed characters: alphanumerics, underscore, dot, slash, hyphen. Traversal ('..') and doubled-slash ('//') are rejected at the Go validator layer.

description.description: What the finding is and why it matters.

remediation.description: Concrete instruction for the craftsman — what to change and how.

type.description: Class of edit. Drives the craftsman's strategy: fix=code change, delete=remove dead code, wire=connect existing-but-orphaned code, rename=symbol/type rename, scrub=remove sensitive output, guard=add nil/range check, doc=update docs/comments.

blast_radius.description: Estimate of fix scope. Used by parallel iterate to schedule co-located fixes serially.

source.description: Originating audit (e.g. audit-security, audit-tests). Survives aggregation.

item.description: Optional symbol or identifier (function, type, route) that the finding is about. Survives aggregation.

deferred.description: Valid findings that are out of scope for this run (pre-existing, too big, or blocked).

follow_up.description: Optional suggested follow-up action (e.g. 'file an issue').

rejected.description: Findings dismissed as false positives or duplicates.

design_questions.description: Optional. Findings that are valid but require a design decision before any code change can be made. Surfaced as a separate 'Design Questions' section in the PR comment so a maintainer can answer them inline. Distinct from `deferred` (which means out-of-scope-for-this-run with no question pending).

id.description: Stable identifier for the design question within the run (e.g. dq1, dq2).

description.description: What the underlying finding is and why it matters.

question.description: The specific decision the maintainer needs to make before this can be actioned.

source_finding_id.description: Optional reference to an aggregated-findings.json entry id this design question was derived from. Null when the question is not tied to a specific source finding.

suggested_followup.description: Optional concrete next step the triage step proposes once the design question is answered (e.g. 'open a follow-up issue', 'document the threat model').
